query: What is the current state of the 2026 Maine Senate race between Susan Collins and Democratic candidates? Who is the Democratic nominee or frontrunner, and what do recent head-to-head polls show as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line on the 2026 Maine Senate race as of July 13, 2026:

**The race has been upended.** Democrat Graham Platner won the June 9 primary but withdrew July 8–10 amid sexual assault allegations (including a rape accusation). **There is currently no Democratic nominee** — Maine Democrats must pick a replacement at a ~600-delegate nominating convention by **July 27, 2026**.

**No clear frontrunner yet.** Declared/floated replacements include Jordan Wood, Secretary of State Shenna Bellows, former Maine CDC Director Nirav Shah, and former state Senate President Troy Jackson.

**Latest head-to-head polling (NOTUS/314 Action, July 9, post-withdrawal)** shows Collins essentially tied with all three tested Democrats: Shah 47–46, Bellows 47–47, Jackson 47–48 (all within the margin of error). Earlier Collins-vs-Platner polling had Platner leading in spring, tightening to a dead heat by late June.

**Ratings & markets:** Cook rates it Toss-up; Inside Elections Tilt Republican. Polymarket's leading (Republican/Collins) outcome jumped to ~63.5% after Platner's exit, up from ~55% in early July, reflecting the Democratic disruption.

Full synthesis, data tables, sources, and structured data points have been delivered above.
